title: CAD, drawing and architectural technicians
description: Research, study, and prepare maps and other spatial data in digital or graphic form for one or more purposes, such as legal, social, political, educational, and design purposes. May work with Geographic Information Systems (GIS). May design and evaluate algorithms, data structures, and user interfaces for GIS and mapping systems. May collect, analyze, and interpret geographic information provided by geodetic surveys, aerial photographs, and satellite data. Make exact measurements and determine property boundaries. Provide data relevant to the shape, contour, gravitation, location, elevation, or dimension of land or land features on or near the earth's surface for engineering, mapmaking, mining, land evaluation, construction, and other purposes. Measure large areas of the Earth's surface using satellite observations, global navigation satellite systems (GNSS), light detection and ranging (LIDAR), or related sources. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct research in surveying and mapping methods, using knowledge of photogrammetric map compilation and electronic data processing.
Verify the accuracy of survey data, including measurements and calculations conducted at survey sites.
Search legal records, survey records, and land titles to obtain information about property boundaries in areas to be surveyed.
Plan and conduct ground surveys designed to establish baselines, elevations, and other geodetic measurements.
Write descriptions of property boundary surveys for use in deeds, leases, or other legal documents.
Prepare, or supervise preparation of, all data, charts, plots, maps, records, and documents related to surveys.
Record the results of surveys, including the shape, contour, location, elevation, and dimensions of land or land features.
Prepare and maintain sketches, maps, reports, and legal descriptions of surveys to describe and assume liability for work performed.
Conduct surveys to determine exact positions, elevations, lines, areas, volumes, contours, or other features of land surfaces.
Analyze survey objectives and specifications to prepare survey proposals or direct others in survey proposal preparation.
Review existing standards, controls, or equipment used, recommending changes or upgrades as needed.
Perform calculations to determine earth curvature corrections, atmospheric impacts on measurements, and placement of markers.
Collect information needed to carry out new surveys, using source maps, previous survey data, or photographs.
Conduct surveys to ascertain the locations of natural features and man-made structures using GPS and other surveying instruments.
Enter GPS data, legal deeds, and field notes into GIS workstations to transform information into graphic land descriptions.
Prepare topographic or contour maps of surveyed land, including relevant site features such as charts, drawings, and survey notes.
Record survey measurements or descriptive data using notes, drawings, sketches, or tracings.
Search for section corners, property irons, or survey points to assist in land surveys.
Set out and recover stakes or other monumentation as part of land surveys.
Supervise or coordinate activities of workers engaged in surveying, plotting data, or drafting maps.
Compare topographical features with images from aerial photographs or old maps to verify accuracy.
Provide assistance in developing methods and procedures for conducting field surveys.
Generate maps that denote specific land locations or geographic attributes by tracing contours or topographic details.
Calculate angles, areas, and other information for mapmaking using survey field notes or reference tables.
Monitor mapping work to ensure accuracy and compliance with rules and regulations.
Produce or update overlay maps to show information boundaries or topographic features.
Compile information necessary to stake projects for construction using engineering plans.
Identify and compile database information to create requested maps for various purposes.
Check map layers to ensure accuracy, marking errors and making corrections where necessary.
Operate and manage land-information computer systems, storing data and producing reports.
Analyze aerial photographs to detect significant topographical data or land features.
Research existing property information to describe boundaries in relation to adjacent properties.

Use drone technology to capture aerial views and topographical data for civil engineering projects.

Operate drones for site surveying and inspection, providing detailed aerial views of project sites.

Deploy and monitor drones for aerial surveillance and assessment of fire situations.
